message:
fix: prevent u128→u64 truncation in Duration-to-nanos conversions

Multiple call sites used `duration.as_nanos() as u64` which silently
truncates when the Duration exceeds ~584 years (u64::MAX nanoseconds).
While rare in practice, Duration::MAX is a valid sentinel value used in
timeout configurations, and truncation there produces silently wrong
deadlines instead of the intended "effectively infinite" timeout.

Changes by module:

- rate_limit: BlockWithTimeout deadline computation now uses
  u64::try_from(timeout.as_millis()).unwrap_or(u64::MAX) with
  saturating_add, preventing overflow on huge timeouts. Added test
  verifying Duration::MAX produces a u64::MAX deadline.

- jetstream: Extracted duration_to_nanos_saturating() helper and
  compute_client_deadline() function. The inline timeout→deadline
  arithmetic is replaced with the safe helper. Added tests for
  Duration::MAX saturation and large-timeout deadline computation.

- reactor/lab: Extracted local duration_to_nanos_saturating() and
  replaced all 5 occurrences of `duration.as_nanos() as u64` in
  inject_event(), advance_time(), poll(), and the chaos-delay path.
  Added 3 tests: saturation unit test, inject_event with Duration::MAX,
  and poll timeout with Duration::MAX.

- deadline_monitor: Replaced the wall-clock-only check_interval gate
  with a dual-clock approach: the monitor now tracks both logical time
  (Time) and wall-clock time (Instant), and allows a scan when either
  clock shows the interval has elapsed. This fixes a subtle issue where
  paused logical time could starve deadline checks indefinitely.
  Extracted duration_to_nanos() helper for the interval comparison.
  Added two tests: one verifying logical-time-driven scan triggering,
  another verifying wall-clock fallback when logical time is stable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
